Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq transcriptomes of Glycyrrhiza uralensis roots, cortexes, and leaves; NCBI SRA BioProject PRJNA945816
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Liquiritin apioside shows potent antitussive activity; flavonoid apiosides are described as bioactive natural products and believed to play hormone-like roles in plant growth regulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression analysis; RT-PCR cloning; heterologous expression in E. coli; in vitro enzyme assay with UAXS-based UDP-apiose supply system; LC/MS and reference standards; kinetic analysis; substrate promiscuity profiling; scaled-up reactions with NMR structure elucidation; site-directed mutagenesis; crystal structure analysis; MD/metadynamics/QM-MM calculations; HDX-MS; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana with metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Roots of Glycyrrhiza uralensis (higher liquiritin apioside and isoliquiritin apioside contents than cortex and leaves)
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Some divalent cations suppressed catalytic activity; no plant growth/yield penalty reported
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Flavonoid glycosides, especially liquiritin and isoliquiritin; sugar donor UDP-apiose
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Family 1 plant UDP-dependent glycosyltransferase (UGT79B74); GT-B fold; PSPG box of 45 amino acids with RLGSDH sugar-binding motif
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers an apiosyl group from UDP-apiose to the 2''-OH of sugar moieties on flavonoid glycosides, producing 2''-O-apiosides such as liquiritin apioside and isoliquiritin apioside
Pathway_Genes.How_Gene_Affects_Metabolite: Promotes biosynthesis of flavonoid apiosides by catalyzing the missing apiosylation step
Pathway_Genes.Promoters: 35S promoter used for transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; native promoter NA
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Highly expressed in roots; expression pattern similar to GuCHS and GuCHR
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Almost completely converted liquiritin and isoliquiritin into their 2''-O-apiosides; enabled de novo production of at least 10 flavonoid apiosides in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro assays at pH 8.0 and 37 degrees C; transient Agrobacterium-mediated expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; UDP-GlcA/UAXS used to supply UDP-apiose
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Liquiritin apioside; isoliquiritin apioside; other flavonoid apiosides
Pathway_Genes.Main_Findings: 1. GuApiGT is the missing phenolic apiosyltransferase in Glycyrrhiza uralensis flavonoid apioside biosynthesis. 2. It efficiently and regio-selectively catalyzes 2''-O-apiosylation of flavonoid glycosides with strict preference for UDP-apiose. 3. The unique 45-aa PSPG box and RLGSDH motif underlie sugar donor selectivity. 4. GuApiGT enabled de novo biosynthesis of flavonoid apiosides in tobacco.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Useful for metabolic engineering and bio-production of bioactive flavonoid apiosides such as liquiritin apioside in heterologous plant systems
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Named UGT79B74; ORF 1365 bp encoding 454 aa; optimum activity at pH 8.0 and 37 degrees C; Km for isoliquiritin 2.59 ± 0.23 umol/L; PDB 8HZZ
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key missing terminal biosynthetic step; rate-limiting not established

Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Candidate identified by homology search of online plant transcriptome databases; accession OQ230795
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Flavonoid apiosides are bioactive natural products
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene cloning; heterologous expression; enzyme catalysis assay; LC/MS functional identification
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Family 1 plant UDP-dependent glycosyltransferase with 45-aa PSPG box and RLGSDH-like motif
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes apiosyl transfer from UDP-apiose to flavonoid glycosides to form flavonoid apiosides
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Identified as an apiosyltransferase by enzyme catalysis reactions
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro enzyme assay with UAXS-based UDP-apiose supply
Pathway_Genes.Main_Findings: 1. PtApiGT is one of four Leguminosae enzymes experimentally confirmed as apiosyltransferases. 2. It supports that the RLGSDH-containing 45-aa PSPG box marks Leguminosae ApiGTs.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Potential tool for engineering flavonoid apioside biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of 121 candidate ApiGTs discovered from 39 Leguminosae species

Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Candidate identified by homology search of online plant transcriptome databases; accession OQ230794

Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene synthesis/cloning; heterologous expression; enzyme catalysis assay; LC/MS functional identification
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Family 1 plant UDP-dependent glycosyltransferase with 45-aa PSPG box and RLGSDH-like motif
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes apiosyl transfer from UDP-apiose to flavonoid glycosides to form flavonoid apiosides
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Identified as an apiosyltransferase by enzyme catalysis reactions
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro enzyme assay with UAXS-based UDP-apiose supply
Pathway_Genes.Main_Findings: 1. SsApiGT is one of four Leguminosae enzymes experimentally confirmed as apiosyltransferases. 2. It supports generality of the 45-aa PSPG box/RLGSDH motif in Leguminosae ApiGTs.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Potential tool for engineering flavonoid apioside biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Full length gene synthesized for experiments

Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Candidate identified by homology search of online plant transcriptome databases; accession OQ230797
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Flavonoid apiosides are bioactive natural products
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene cloning; heterologous expression; enzyme catalysis assay; LC/MS functional identification
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Family 1 plant UDP-dependent glycosyltransferase with highly conserved ApiGT sequence and 45-aa PSPG box
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes apiosyl transfer from UDP-apiose to flavonoid glycosides to form flavonoid apiosides
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Identified as an apiosyltransferase by enzyme catalysis reactions
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro enzyme assay with UAXS-based UDP-apiose supply
Pathway_Genes.Main_Findings: 1. GgApiGT is a confirmed Leguminosae apiosyltransferase. 2. It supports conservation of apiosylation function among Glycyrrhiza species.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Potential resource for apioside pathway engineering in licorice relatives
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Amino acid sequences of the four new ApiGTs are highly conserved with 90.07% identity

Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Candidate identified by homology search of online plant transcriptome databases; accession OQ230796
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Flavonoid apiosides are bioactive natural products
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene cloning; heterologous expression; enzyme catalysis assay; LC/MS functional identification
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Family 1 plant UDP-dependent glycosyltransferase with highly conserved ApiGT sequence and 45-aa PSPG box
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes apiosyl transfer from UDP-apiose to flavonoid glycosides to form flavonoid apiosides
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Identified as an apiosyltransferase by enzyme catalysis reactions
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro enzyme assay with UAXS-based UDP-apiose supply
Pathway_Genes.Main_Findings: 1. GiApiGT is a confirmed Leguminosae apiosyltransferase. 2. It supports conservation of apiosylation function among Glycyrrhiza species.

Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Discovered from Glycyrrhiza uralensis transcriptome co-expression analysis; accession OQ266890; RNA-seq BioProject PRJNA945816
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Provides liquiritin and isoliquiritin intermediates required for downstream bioactive apioside formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression analysis; gene cloning; transient pathway engineering in Nicotiana benthamiana; enzyme assay and LC/MS functional analysis
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Using alternative GuGT14 caused major byproduct 7a due to poor regioselectivity and endogenous tobacco glycosyltransferases; GuGT53 improved product specificity
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Liquiritigenin; isoliquiritigenin; likely UDP-glucose as sugar donor
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Regio-selectively and efficiently catalyzes 4'/4-O-glycosylation of liquiritigenin and isoliquiritigenin to form liquiritin and isoliquiritin
Pathway_Genes.How_Gene_Affects_Metabolite: Supplies direct glycosylated precursors for GuApiGT-mediated apioside biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Promoters: pEAQ-HT-DEST1 vector used for transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; promoter context 35S-based pEAQ system
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Expression pattern similar to GuCHS, GuCHR, and GuApiGT
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Improved isoliquiritin apioside production when replacing GuGT14 in the tobacco pathway because of superior regioselectivity
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Agrobacterium-mediated transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Flavonoid glycosylation branch upstream of apiosylation
Pathway_Genes.Main_Findings: 1. GuGT53 is a regio-selective glycosyltransferase from G. uralensis. 2. It converts liquiritigenin and isoliquiritigenin into liquiritin and isoliquiritin, respectively. 3. Replacing GuGT14 with GuGT53 improved apioside production in tobacco by reducing byproduct formation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Useful for pathway reconstruction and metabolic engineering of liquiritin/isoliquiritin-derived apiosides

Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Structural data only; PDB 8IOE for Sb3GT1-UDP complex and 8IOD for mutant 375S/Q377H-UDP-Glc complex
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Mutant forms kaempferol 3-O-apioside; demonstrates engineering of sugar-donor selectivity in flavonoid glycosylation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Crystal structure determination; site-directed mutagenesis; enzyme assays with UAXS-based UDP-apiose supply system; LC/MS product detection
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Wild-type Sb3GT1 cannot use UDP-apiose; apiosylation only gained after motif engineering
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Kaempferol; native enzyme accepts multiple sugar donors except UDP-apiose
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Family 1 plant UDP-dependent glycosyltransferase; flavonoid 3-O-glycosyltransferase; FFGDQ motif in WT mapped to RLGSDH region of GuApiGT
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Wild type catalyzes flavonoid 3-O-glycosylation; engineered mutants acquired the ability to transfer apiose to kaempferol to form kaempferol 3-O-apioside
Pathway_Genes.How_Gene_Affects_Metabolite: Engineered motif changes enable apioside formation from a non-apiosylating UGT scaffold
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Mutants 375S/Q377H, F372R/Q376H, and F372R/375S/Q377H gained apiosylation activity toward kaempferol, proving the importance of the RLGSDH-like motif for UDP-apiose recognition
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro assays at pH 9.0 and 45 degrees C with UAXS-generated UDP-apiose
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Kaempferol 3-O-apioside; wild-type native glycosides not detailed here
Pathway_Genes.Main_Findings: 1. Sb3GT1 is a non-apiosylating flavonoid 3-O-glycosyltransferase scaffold. 2. Engineering its FFGDQ-region toward the GuApiGT motif conferred apiosylation activity. 3. This validates the critical role of the expanded motif architecture in UDP-apiose selectivity.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Provides a template for protein engineering of UGT sugar-donor selectivity to synthesize novel flavonoid apiosides
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Shares 19.8% amino acid identity with GuApiGT

Title: Insights into the missing apiosylation step in flavonoid apiosides biosynthesis of Leguminosae plants
